content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission
May 12,
1994
Robert J.
Lanzone
Office of the City Attorney
City of San Carlos
939 Laurel Street, Suite
D
P.O.
Box 1065
San Carlos,
CA
94070
Re:
Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-94-133
Dear Mr.
Lanzone:
This is in response to your letter requesting advice regard
ing San Carlos City Councilmember Torn Davids and his obligations
and responsibilities under the conflict—of—interest provisions of
the Political Reform Act
(the “Act”).
1
OUESTION
Councilmember Davids is an employee of the San Mateo County
Transit District
(“SAMTRANS”).
May Councilmember Davids
participate in decisions pertaining to the grade separation
project,
which is a joint project between the city and SAMTRANS?
CONCLUSION
Under the provisions of the Act,
Councilmember Davids may
participate in decisions pertaining to the grade separation
project.
FACTS
The City of San Carlos has undertaken a project to create a
grade separation for a portion of the Southern Pacific Right-of-
Way as it passes through the city.
The city has entered into an
agreement entitled “Grade Separation Development Agreement” with
the San Mateo Transportation Authority,
a public corporation,
and
the San Mateo County Transit District,
a public agency.
In
addition, the city and
SAMTRANS have entered into an agreement and
1
Government Code Sections 81000-91015.
All statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission
regulations appear at
2 Ca1iforniaCodf Regulations,
Sections
18000—18954.
All references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division
6 of the California Code of Regulations.
428 J Street, Suite 800 •
P.O. Box 807 •
Sacramento, CA
95804-0807 •
(916) 322-5660


File No.
A—94—133
Page
2
purchased approximately 5.1 acres of right—of—way from Southern
Pacific as of June 18,
1992.
Pursuant to the above agreements, the city will,
as a lead
agency, construct the actual grade separation.
The city will be
working with SNTRANS during this two to three year project.
In
addition, the city will be in a joint ownership,
as tenants in
common,
of the 5.1 acres of land.
Presently, there are approx
imately ten tenants on the property;
some of the tenants will be
relocated as part of the Grade Separation Project and some of the
tenants will remain.
SANTRANS will manage the property.
Tom Davids was elected to the city council in November 1991.
He has been a full-time salaried employee of
SANTRANS since
approximately July 1,
1991.
Prior to his employment with
SAMTRANS, he had been under contract with
SAMTRANS to manage a
large office building at 1250 San Carlos Avenue,
in the City of
San Carlos, where
SAMTRANS has its headquarters.
Mr. Davids’ duties with
SZJNTRANS include a broad area of
property management with respect to the grade separation project.
Specifically,
Mr.
Davids manages the property that has been
acquired from Southern Pacific and he will manage the leases with
the tenants on the property.
After the grade separation is completed, it is anticipated
that the property may be returned fully to ownership of
SAMTRANS.
Mr.
Davids anticipates a lead role regarding recommendation for
the development of any surplus land on the 5.1 acres.
Mr.
Davids also anticipates having a role in reviewing
contract documentation for contractors employed by the city or the
city’s general contractor on the grade separation project.
ANALYSIS
The Act prohibits a public official from making, participat
ing in making,
or in any way attempting to use his or her official
position to influence a governmental decision in which the
official knows or has reason to know he or she has a financial
interest.
(Section 87100.)
An official has a financial interest
in a decision if the decision will have a reasonably foreseeable
material financial effect, distinguishable from the effect on the
public generally,
on the official or a member of his or her
immediate family or on:
* ** * *
(C)
Any source of income, oJir than gifts
and other than loans by a commercial lending
institution in the regular course of business on
terms available to the public without regard to
official status, aggregating two hundred fifty
dollars
($250)
or more in value provided to,


File No.
A—94—133
Page
3
received by or promised to the public official
within 12 months prior to the time when the
decision is made.
(d)
Any business entity in which the public
official is a director, officer, partner, trustee,
employee, or holds any position of management.
Section 87103(c)
and (d).
(Emphasis added.)
Mr.
Davids is employed by SANTRANS,
a nonprofit public
agency.
Salary and reimbursement for expenses or per diem
received from a state, local or federal government agency is
expressly exempted from the definition of “income” for purposes of
the Act.
(Section 82030(b)(2).)
Thus,
SAMTRANS is not considered
a “source of income” to Mr.
Davids for purposes of the disclosure
or disqualification provisions of the Act.
Furthermore, since
SAMTRANS is not considered a source of income, the provisions for
“nexus” do not apply.
(Regulation 18702.1(a) (1).)
In addition, because
S.AMTRANS is not an organization or
enterprise operated for profit, it is not a “business entity” as
defined by Section 82005 of the Act and Section 87103(d)
is not
applicable.
Therefore,
Mr.
Davids’ status as an employee of
SANTRANS
does not create a conflict of interest concerning city council
decisions pertaining to the grade separation project which will
affect
SANTRANS.
Accordingly,
Mr.
Davids may participate in
decisions pertaining to the grade separation project.
Please note that our advice is limited to the provisions of
the Political Reform Act.
In regard to your question as to
whether Mr. Davids has a conflict as a
SANTRANS employee,
in
working for
SANTRANS on the grade separation project,
we suggest
that you contact the Office of the Attorney General to ascertain
if any other provisions of law,
such as the doctrine of
incompatible activities,
may apply to this situation.
I trust this answers your question.
Sincerely,
Steven G.
Churchwell
General Counsel
By:
Stecher
sel, Legal Division
SGC: JS : ak

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Councilmember Davids is an employee of the San Mateo County
Transit District
(“SAMTRANS”).
May Councilmember Davids
participate in decisions pertaining to the grade separation
project,
which is a joint project between the city and SAMTRANS?

CONCLUSION: Under the provisions of the Act,
Councilmember Davids may
participate in decisions pertaining to the grade separation
project.
